danger_signals.visual_form: Creature sprites and attack animations in first-person view
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring evasion or combat

danger_signals.visual_form: Dungeon geometry, dark atmospheric areas, elemental visual themes (fire, water, ice)
Large ornate chamber with Korax (the Serpent Rider) visible, distinctive architecture
danger_signals.meaning: Major danger threshold; significant combat encounter ahead

danger_signals.visual_form: UI health indicator depletion, character visual damage feedback
danger_signals.meaning: Player vulnerability; need for healing items

safety_signals.visual_form: Central area with multiple doorways/exits to side levels, generally calmer architecture
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone for puzzle progression and item gathering

safety_signals.visual_form: Glowing inventory items (flechette, Disc of Repulsion, Icon of the Defender), visual sparkle effects
Empty rooms after enemy defeat, doors now open

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Demonic/dark magic aesthetic; Serpent Rider imagery
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Various creature types with dark, corrupted visual design
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Necropolis (final area); dark dungeons; elemental corruption zones

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Class-specific weapon and armor designs (Fighter/Cleric/Mage distinct gear)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mountainside seminary; sanctuary spaces within dungeons

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Overrun dungeons, corrupted elemental zones, necropolis dominance, dark atmospheric rendering
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively patrol and attack; environmental hazards present

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple doorways in hub areas; item placements indicate solution paths; polyobject (moving wall) animations when activated
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hub doors remain sealed until items/switches activated; side levels open as prerequisites met

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fighter (armored, physical), Cleric (robed, balanced), Mage (mystical, fragile) visual distinctions in weapon design and spell effects
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Fighter fast/tough melee; Mage slow/fragile ranged spells; Cleric hybrid combat style

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Progression through elemental dungeons → wilderness → seminary → castle → necropolis
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environment shifts from elemental (fire/ice/water themes) through natural landscape to structured architecture to death imagery
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy variety and difficulty increases; hazard complexity escalates

environmental_storytelling.example: Player moves from elemental dungeons (primal corruption) through wilderness (reclaimed land) to monastery (civilization) to dark castle (Korax's stronghold) to necropolis (death realm), showing invasion scope
Magical artifacts (Disc of Repulsion, Icon of the Defender) scattered throughout world suggest civilization's last defense efforts against Korax
Central hub areas with locked exits requiring items from side quests communicate puzzle dependencies and world-scale threat requiring coordinated hero action
Creature variety and placement in elemental vs. structural zones communicates different aspects of Korax's corruption influence across the realm

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Fast movement, melee weapon attacks, higher durability; visual priority is forward momentum and sword/axe swing animations
Balanced speed, melee + ranged attacks, medium durability; visual blend of close-combat and spell-casting animations
Slow movement, long-range spell projectiles, low durability; visual focus on magical effect animations and spell impact
Commanding presence in ornate necropolis arena; attacks from elevated position; visual demonic power manifestation
Wander until player detected; activate attack sequences; visual tells: movement toward player, attack animation initiation
Translate, rotate, or move when script/switch activated; visual movement indicates puzzle progress or path opening

notes: Hexen uses a modified Doom engine with Action Code Script (ACS) for complex environmental changes and state communication. The three-character system creates distinct visual/behavioral communication of player capability. Hub-level design allows temporal world-state changes: locked areas become accessible, enemy placement may change, environmental effects (earthquakes) manipulate visibility. The progression from elemental dungeons through structured civilization to necropolis creates visual narrative of escalating invasion. Class-specific item behavior (e.g., flechette poison gas) means identical pickups communicate differently based on player state. Polyobjects enable moving wall visual feedback for puzzle progression without traditional door sprites.
